message:
br-bd-8xbt0 EXT4_IOC_PRECACHE_EXTENTS ioctl: extent metadata read-ahead hint

ext4_ext_precache (fs/ext4/ioctl.c) walks the on-disk extent tree
for an inode and pulls every internal/leaf block into the page
cache so subsequent reads don't stall on metadata I/O. e2fsck pass
1, debugfs dump_extents, and certain backup tools issue
EXT4_IOC_PRECACHE_EXTENTS = _IO('f', 38) = 0x6626 before walking
large fragmented files. Pre-fix dispatch_ioctl returned ENOTTY
because the FUSE handler had no branch.

ffs-core surface:
- New `FsOps::precache_extents(cx, scope, ino) -> Result<()>` trait
  method (default Ok(()) — best-effort hint contract).
- OpenFs override: ext4 reads the inode (validates existence), then
  walks `collect_extents_with_scope` if EXT4_EXTENTS_FL is set —
  the side effect of that walk is that ffs-extent's per-scope
  block cache populates every internal/leaf block exactly once,
  matching the kernel's precache effect. Block-mapped legacy
  inodes (no EXT4_EXTENTS_FL) are a successful no-op, matching
  ext4_ext_precache returning 0 for non-extent inodes. btrfs reads
  the inode (extent items are inline in the FS tree, so the inode
  read already touches the same blocks ext4_ext_precache would
  warm) and returns Ok.

ffs-fuse wiring:
- EXT4_IOC_PRECACHE_EXTENTS = 0x0000_6626 (no input/output payload;
  it's _IO with size=0).
- dispatch_ioctl branch ignores in_data + out_size (per _IO
  contract), runs `ops.precache_extents(...)` under
  RequestOp::IoctlRead scope, and returns IoctlResult::Data(empty)
  on success or IoctlResult::Error(errno) on backend failure.
- IoctlRecordingFs gains an IoctlCall::PrecacheExtents(InodeNumber)
  trace variant + matching `precache_extents` impl.

Tests: 708 ffs-core + 285 ffs-fuse pass. New regressions:
- dispatch_ioctl_precache_extents_routes_to_fsops_with_empty_reply:
  pins success path through with_request_scope, asserts
  Begin(IoctlRead) + PrecacheExtents(ino) + End(IoctlRead) trace.
- dispatch_ioctl_precache_extents_ignores_input_payload_and_out_size:
  proves the dispatcher mirrors kernel _IO semantics (any non-zero
  in_data length or out_size is accepted, never read).

Refs: bd-8xbt0
